task_context.actionable_question: Am I a procrastinator and is it costing me marks?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Use submission_delay_avg [FE]. Identify if late submission is systematic. Link to score.

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: It appears that you have not submitted any assessments yet, which indicates a pattern of procrastination. This lack of submissions is concerning as it directly impacts your scores and overall engagement with the course.

Insights: No Submissions Recorded: You have not submitted any of the assessments, which suggests a consistent pattern of procrastination. This is reflected in your punctuality rate of 0, indicating that you have not submitted on time or at all. | Impact on Scores: Your scores for the assessments you attempted are low, with a normalized score of 55 for both G2 and G3. This suggests that even when you do engage, the lack of timely submissions may hinder your performance.

Educational implications: Timely submissions are crucial for maintaining good scores and understanding course material.

Recommendations: high - Set specific deadlines for yourself to complete assessments before the due dates. - Creating personal deadlines can help you manage your time better and reduce procrastination. | medium - Engage with course materials regularly to build a routine. - Regular engagement can help you stay on track and improve your understanding, leading to better performance.

explanation.summary: It appears that you have not submitted any assessments yet, which indicates a pattern of procrastination. This lack of submissions is concerning as it directly impacts your scores and overall engagement with the course.

insights.title: No Submissions Recorded
insights.description: You have not submitted any of the assessments, which suggests a consistent pattern of procrastination. This is reflected in your punctuality rate of 0, indicating that you have not submitted on time or at all.

insights.title: Impact on Scores
insights.description: Your scores for the assessments you attempted are low, with a normalized score of 55 for both G2 and G3. This suggests that even when you do engage, the lack of timely submissions may hinder your performance.

educational_implications: Timely submissions are crucial for maintaining good scores and understanding course material.

recommendations.action: Set specific deadlines for yourself to complete assessments before the due dates.
recommendations.rationale: Creating personal deadlines can help you manage your time better and reduce procrastination.

recommendations.action: Engage with course materials regularly to build a routine.
recommendations.rationale: Regular engagement can help you stay on track and improve your understanding, leading to better performance.

confidence.reason: The data clearly shows no submissions and low scores, indicating a strong pattern of procrastination.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify whether late submission is systematic.
Describe the observed relationship between submission delay and score.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use submission_delay_avg as the primary delay metric; do not infer procrastination from score alone.
Frame the delay-score relationship as correlational, not causal.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
